query: Draft a suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral joint family property situated at Bangalore. The Plaintiff and Defendants are Hindu co-owners who inherited the property from their deceased father. The property includes a residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore measuring 2400 sq.ft. and agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural measuring 2 acres. The Defendants are denying the Plaintiff's rightful 1/3rd share and refusing to partition. Claim mesne profits for exclusion from possession. Include genealogy table, schedule of properties, and prayer for appointment of Commissioner. Draft suitable for filing before the City Civil Court, Bangalore.

v4.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BANGALORE

Partition Suit for declaration of 1/3 share in ancestral joint family property and for partition and separate possession, with mesne profits for exclusion from possession, and appointment of Commissioner

PLAINTIFF: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS
DEFENDANT: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, Aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}. ... DEFENDANT

JURISDICTION

country: India; state: Karnataka; city: Bangalore; court_type: City Civil Court; place: null. This Hon'ble Court has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain the partition suit filed by the Plaintiff, a Hindu coparcener, in respect of ancestral joint family property situate at Bangalore, comprising residential house No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore (2400 sq.ft.) and agricultural land Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural (2 acres). The cause of action arose within the local limits of this Court, and the value of the subject matter falls within the pecuniary limits of this Court.

FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff and Defendants are members of a Hindu family and hold the property as co-owners in joint family status, each entitled to an undivided share in the ancestral estate inherited from their father, {{FATHER_NAME}}, who is deceased.

2. The Father owned and possessed the residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore measuring 2400 sq.ft. and agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural measuring 2 acres.

3. The Plaintiff claims an equal 1/3 share in the aforesaid joint family property by succession, as the coparcenary rights are recognized under law in the relevant enacting statutes.

4. The Defendants are denying the Plaintiff's 1/3 share and refusing to partition the joint ancestral property, thereby obstructing rightful Partition and Possession.

5. The Plaintiff has on numerous occasions elicited a willingness for partition, but the Defendants have not taken steps to effect partition or grant possession of the Plaintiff's share.

6. The Plaintiff has been continuously prevented from enjoying or occupying his 1/3 share by the Defendants, who have asserted exclusive possession of the entire property.

7. In order to protect the Plaintiff's rights and to obtain a fair and just partition, a Commissioner may be necessary to divide the property and to delineate each co-owner's share, as provided under the applicable Civil Procedure rules.

8. The Plaintiff has prepared and annexed Annexure A (Genealogy Table) and Annexure B (Schedule of Properties) to illustrate the family tree and the properties subject to partition.

9. Copies of the title documents, revenue records, and other proofs of ownership are annexed as Annexure C to establish the joint ownership status and the boundaries of the two assets.

10. The Plaintiff seeks appointment of a Commissioner to carry out a physical partition and to deliver possession of the Plaintiff's 1/3 share, along with a declaration of rights and possession.

11. The Plaintiff further seeks mesne profits for the period of exclusion from possession as a consequential relief, to be determined by the Court after due inquiry, in accordance with Order XX Rule 18 CPC and related provisions.

12. For reference, Annexure A to Annexure C are annexed herewith and marked accordingly.

13. The Plaintiff asserts that the partition and possession are essential to establish and protect the Plaintiff's legal rights as a co-owner.

14. The Plaintiff asserts that there is no valid partition in effect as of the filing of this Suit and that the Defendants have not furnished any partition plan or accounts.

15. This Suit is being filed on the grounds of a continuing cause of action, as the right to partition exists from the time of the father's death and continues until final partition and possession are effected.

LEGAL BASIS

16. Order XX Rule 18 CPC enables a co-tenant or co-owner to file a suit for partition and separate possession, and to appoint a Commissioner to effect such partition where the property is indivisible in kind.

17. The Hindu Succession Act, 1956 (Sections 6, 8, 10) provides coparcenary rights, devolution, and succession among heirs to ancestral property, which support the Plaintiff's entitlement to a 1/3 share.

18. The Partition Act, 1893 (Sections 2, 3, 4) governs partition of joint family or coparcenary property and empowers courts to adjudicate and partition such property among co-owne {{AMOUNT}}. The Indian Succession Act, 1925 (Sections 211, 218) governs devolution of property and can support equitable partition among heirs where necessary.

19. The provisions cited are to be read together to grant a fair partition and to empower the court to appoint a Commissioner to execute the partition and deliver possession of the Plaintiff's 1/3 share, along with any other appropriate relief including mesne profits for exclusion from possession.

CAUSE OF ACTION

20. ORIGIN: The cause of action first arose on the death of the father, {{FATHER_DEATH_DATE}}, when the ancestral property devolved upon the Plaintiff and Defendants as co-owners, and the Plaintiff's rightful 1/3 share became exigible to partition.

21. FURTHER ACCRUAL: The cause of action further accrued on an ongoing basis due to the Defendants' denial of the Plaintiff's share and refusal to partition or grant possession, with continued exclusion and denial of the Plaintiff's rights.

22. CONTINUING NATURE: The cause of action is continuing in nature, as the Defendants continue to withhold possession and refuse partition, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of his rightful 1/3 share and the benefits of co-ownership; the Plaintiff's right to partition subsists and continues to accrue till final decree and actual partition and possession.

LIMITATION

No general period of limitation governs partition suits between co-owners. This filing is made in reliance on the ongoing nature of the right to partition and possession; if ouster or exclusion is pleaded, accrual may be subject to the relevant facts, but there is no fixed limitation period stated herein.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

The suit is governed by ad valorem Court Fee, as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act,

23. The value of the Plaintiff's share for court-fee purposes shall be determined on the market value of the 1/3 share of the ancestral property. The exact court fee payable shall be computed in accordance with the Act and schedule applicable to partition suits; the value and fee shall be recorded in the final computation sheet. The Plaintiff proposes to pay the requisite court fee after valuation of the 1/3 share, and the Court may determine the exact fee at the time of admission.

PRAYER

24. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be graciously pleased to: (a) Declare the Plaintiff's 1/3 undivided share in the ancestral joint family property comprising No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore (Residential house) measuring 2400 sq.ft. and the agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural (2 acres), and partition the said property into indivisible shares with separate possession of the Plaintiff's 1/3 share;
(b) Appoint a Commissioner under Order XX Rule 18 CPC to effect the partition and to demarcate the share and grant possession to the Plaintiff of his 1/3 share;
(c) Award mesne profits for the period of exclusion from possession on account of the Defendants' denial of partition, to be determined by the Court upon inquiry;
(d) Costs of suit; and (e) Such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court deems fit.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

A. Genealogy Table (Annexure A) demonstrating the family tree and coparcenary relations; B. Schedule of Properties (Annexure B) detailing No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar house and Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk land; C. Copies of title deeds, RTCs, revenue records and other documents establishing joint ownership (Annexure C); D. Copy of legal notice/communication and service receipts (Annexure D); E. Copy of Defendant's reply (if any) to partition demands or, in absence, a note of non-response (Annexure E).

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby solemnly verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 23 of this plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge based upon the records and documents available with me, that the contents relating to legal submissions are based upon legal advice received and are believed to be correct, that no part of the cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of any other court, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

v5.draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BANGALORE
O.S. No. __________ / 20____

PARTIES

1. PLAINTIFF: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. The Plaintiff is a Hindu coparcener and co-owner of the ancestral property by inheritance from his father, Late {{FATHER_NAME}}.

2. DEFENDANT: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}. The Defendant is a co-owner of the same ancestral property and denies the Plaintiff's right to a 1/3 share and partitions; the Defendant refuses to partition the jointly held property.

SUIT FOR PARTITION AND SEPARATE POSSESSION OF ANCESTRAL JOINT FAMILY PROPERTY AND MESNE PROFITS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

3. This Hon'ble Court has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the ancestral joint family property is situate within the jurisdiction of the City Civil Court, Bangalore, and the cause of action arises within such jurisdiction in relation to the aforesaid property.

FACTS AND EVIDENCE

4. The Plaintiff and the Defendant are Hindu co-owners in an ancestral joint family property inherited from their deceased father, Late {{FATHER_NAME}}. The property comprises (a) a residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore, measuring 2400 sq. ft.; and (b) agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural, measuring 2 acres. A true and complete description is set out in Schedule of Properties at Annexure A attached hereto. Annexure A is filed herewith and marked as Annexure A.

5. The father of the Plaintiff and Defendant, Late {{FATHER_NAME}}, died on {{FATHER_DEATH_DATE}} leaving behind coparceners in the form of the Plaintiff and the Defendant, who have continued to hold the subject properties as coparceners in a joint Hindu family. The Plaintiff asserts a 1/3 share in the joint family properties and seeks partition and separate possession of his share. The Defendants, however, deny the Plaintiff's 1/3 share and obstruct partition.

6. The Plaintiff and Defendant inherited the properties as joint family properties on the death of their father, and the properties are governed by Hindu succession norms; As such, the Plaintiff is entitled to a share in the joint family properties in accordance with Section 6, Section 8 and Section 10 of the Hindu Succession Act, 1956 (as applicable). The Plaintiff seeks partition and separate possession of his 1/3 share in the joint family properties as described in Annexure A.

7. The Plaintiff requests appointment of a Commissioner to effect partition by metes and bounds and to report to the Court, as provided under the Partition Act, 1893 and the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (Order XX Rule 18). The Defendant has refused to partition despite the Plaintiff's demand for the same.

GENEALOGY TABLE

8. GENEALOGY TABLE (Annexure B)

- Late Sri {{FATHER_NAME}} (deceased) — Father of the Plaintiff and the Defendant, who left behind co-parceners.
- {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} — Plaintiff, son of Late Sri {{FATHER_NAME}}.
- {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} — Defendant, son of Late Sri {{FATHER_NAME}}.
- The joint family property described in Annexure A formed the ancestral property of the family and is held by the Plaintiff and Defendant as coparceners.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTIES

9. SCHEDULE OF PROPERTIES: The properties which are the subject of the suit are more fully described in Annexure A, which is attached hereto and marked as Annexure A. Annexure A contains:
- Item 1: Residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore, measuring 2400 sq. ft.
- Item 2: Agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural, measuring 2 acres.

CAUSE OF ACTION

10. The cause of action for this suit first arose on the death of Late Sri {{FATHER_NAME}} and the subsequent co-owners' failure to partition the joint family properties and share. The Defendants have denied the Plaintiff's rightful 1/3 share and refused to partition; the cause of action is continuing as the joint family property remains undivided and Plaintiff continues to be deprived of his rightful share and possession.

LEGAL BASIS

11. The Plaintiff relies on:
- ORDER XX RULE 18, CPC: For partition and separate possession of jointly held property where a share is claimed.
- Hindu Succession Act, 1956: Sections 6, 8, and 10, for coparcenary rights and succession to joint family property.
- Partition Act, 1893: Sections 2, 3, and 4, for the operation and process of partition and appointment of a Commissioner.
- Indian Succession Act, 1925: Sections 211 and 218 (as applicable to succession and devolution of properties among coparceners).

12. The Plaintiff submits that, on the facts stated, the Plaintiff is entitled to an immediate partition of the joint family property and separate possession of his 1/3 share, and the Court should appoint a Commissioner to effect the partition in accordance with law.

LIMITATION

13. NO LIMITATION APPLIES: There is no applicable period of limitation prescribed for partition between co-owners; this suit is filed within a continuum of the co-owners' rights to seek partition. If ouster or exclusion is pleaded, the accrual would be from the date such exclusion becomes known to the Plaintiff; however, no article of the Limitation Act is cited herein as to limitation.

DESCRIPTIVE AND PRACTICAL PROVISIONS

14. The Plaintiff seeks mesne profits for exclusion from possession of Plaintiff's 1/3 share, if any, from the date of exclusion until delivery of possession, and a decree for partition along with allotment of separate possession of the Plaintiff's 1/3 share.

15. INTEREST AND COSTS: The Plaintiff prays for costs of this suit, including court fees and advocate's fees, and for such interest on decretal sums as the Court may deem fit.

APPOINTMENT OF COMMISSIONER

16. The Plaintiff prays for appointment of a Commissioner to effect partition of the property by metes and bounds, to divide the properties and to file a report with the Court, so that a final decree can be drawn up in terms of the partition completed by the Commissioner.

PRAYER

17. The Plaintiff prays that the Court pass the following reliefs:
- (a) Decree of partition and separate possession of Plaintiff's 1/3 share in Annexure A, by metes and bounds, and to grant possession to the Plaintiff of his share.
- (b) Appointment of a Commissioner to effect partition by metes and bounds and to file a report with the Court.
- (c) Mesne profits for exclusion from possession of Plaintiff's 1/3 share from the date of exclusion until delivery of possession, or until partition is completed.
- (d) Costs of this suit including court fees and advocate's fees.
- (e) Any other relief as this Court may deem fit and proper.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

18. Annexure A — Schedule of Properties (Residential house No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore; Agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural).
19. Annexure B — Genealogy Table (family lineage and coparcenary status).
20. Annexure C — Any other documentary evidence (to be furnished during trial as available).

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 20 of this plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge based upon the records and documents available with me, that the contents relating to legal submissions are based upon legal advice received and are believed to be correct, that no part of the cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of any other court, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

                                                        {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

                                                        PLAINTIFF

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
